title: Action sheet replacement with action grid (4-up icon grid)
design_brief: A share-style action grid (Instagram share, Apple's iOS share grid): 4-per-row grid of icon+label actions in a bottom sheet. Reference: iOS share sheet bottom row, Instagram share to.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Linear list of actions when grid is faster to scan
mobile_reasoning: Grid renders faster than a list for ≥6 actions because users scan visually. Each cell is its own Pressable with hit slop ≥44pt. Bottom sheet reuses the rn-002 pattern.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: ActionGrid component embedded in a bottom sheet.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: On tablet, 6 columns.

animation_strategy: Each cell: spring scale on press.
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Hooks are declared once at component top level; mapped marks are plain memoized data, so the Rules of Hooks are preserved.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Per-cell scale on press feels tactile. 2) 4-col grid matches iOS share sheet density.

title: Expandable rows that animate height smoothly inside FlashList
design_brief: A FAQ or email-list where rows expand to show details on tap. Other rows reflow smoothly. Reference: Apple Mail thread expand, FAQ pages.
mobile_reasoning: Each row owns an 'expanded' state and a measured detail height. Use Reanimated layout animations + per-row shared value for height. FlashList tolerates dynamic heights but performs better when expansion is per-cell.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Height: useSharedValue with withSpring on toggle.
interaction_details: Other rows in list slide via Layout.springify
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Layout animations on UI thread. Per-row state.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Reanimated Layout for in-list reflow. 2) Spring physics on expand. 3) Haptic feedback.

title: Currency input with locale-aware formatting
design_brief: A money input field that formats as you type: cents pin to the right, comma separators, currency symbol prefix. Reference: Cash App money entry, Stripe checkout amount.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: String parsing on every keystroke without normalization
Display formatting that fights with user typing
mobile_reasoning: Internally we track an integer (cents). Display via Intl.NumberFormat. User keystrokes interpreted as appending digits.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Font scales down if value too long.

animation_strategy: Validation feedback is subtle and live-region driven; keyboard movement is handled by KeyboardAvoidingView.
gesture_strategy: Tap targets are at least 44px and form submission is explicit; no hidden gesture is required.
interaction_details: Digits append from right, formatted as currency
accessibility_considerations: Each input has a label, error hint, visible validation copy, and disabled submit state.
performance_notes: Intl is native on RN 0.76+ (Hermes intl support). Re-renders on each digit.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Number-pad keyboard. 2) Tabular-nums for stable digit widths. 3) Locale-aware formatting.

title: Deep linking with state restoration
design_brief: App handles deep links like myapp://post/123. Opening a link should navigate to the correct screen with proper back stack so user can navigate back through expected hierarchy. Reference: any well-implemented app's universal links.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Push the destination without setting up the back stack
Ignoring initial URL when app opened from cold
mobile_reasoning: Expo Router auto-generates routes from filesystem and supports deep links via the app.json scheme. Back stack established by visiting parent route first then target.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: app.json defines scheme. Expo Router handles routing.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Universal links require associated domains setup on iOS; deep links on Android require intent filters.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Native.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Tap deep link in another app → opens this app to /post/123
Back navigation walks to / (home) — Expo Router infers from filesystem
Cold start with deep link works (initial URL)
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Universal links / app links — OS-level routing. 2) Expo Router filesystem-based stack inference. 3) Back stack reconstructed naturally.

title: Animated gradient mesh background
design_brief: A subtly animated gradient mesh as a background — soft color blobs drifting and morphing. Reference: Apple Music album backgrounds, Spotify Now Playing.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Complex shader code for simple radial blobs
mobile_reasoning: Skia draws radial gradients per blob, each with its center as a shared value drifting via sine functions over time. Composite with softLight or screen blend modes.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Fills parent.

animation_strategy: Per-blob sine drift via useFrameCallback.
gesture_strategy: Touch is optional; the drawing exposes a semantic image label and avoids gesture-only meaning.
interaction_details: Continuous slow drift of color blobs
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Per-frame callback. Skia BlurMask is GPU. Keep blob count ≤ 5 for old devices.
what_makes_this_native: 1) GPU-rendered gradients. 2) Per-blob shared values driven by single time value. 3) Soft, ambient — never blocks foreground.

title: Progress ring that morphs to checkmark on complete
design_brief: A small progress indicator next to a list row that fills as task completes, then morphs into a checkmark with a spring. Reference: Things 3 task completion, Apple Reminders.
mobile_reasoning: Skia path arc for the ring fill; on complete, animate to a checkmark path via two-phase animation: arc completes → ring fades while checkmark draws.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Fixed.

animation_strategy: progress shared value 0→1 for ring; complete shared value 0→1 for checkmark.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Ring fills progressively as progress prop changes
On complete: ring stays full, checkmark draws over it in two strokes
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityRole='progressbar' accessibilityValue with progress; state 'complete' when done.
performance_notes: Skia paths derived in worklets. Single canvas.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Path morph via shared values. 2) GPU rendering. 3) Two-phase check draw feels considered.

title: Image cropper with drag handles and aspect-ratio presets
design_brief: A photo cropper screen: image visible, dark overlay outside crop box, 8 drag handles (4 corner + 4 edge), aspect ratio toggles (Free, 1:1, 4:5, 16:9). Reference: iOS Photos crop, Instagram crop.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No corner handles (edge-only is hard to use)
Handles too small to grab (need 32+ pt hit area)
Aspect ratio that doesn't constrain the box
mobile_reasoning: Crop box has its own translate + width/height shared values. Handle pans update box dimensions with aspect-ratio constraint if set. Visible image stays put; only crop overlay moves.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Image fits screen; crop box scales with image.

animation_strategy: None during drag; spring snap to aspect ratio on toggle.
interaction_details: Toggle aspect ratio constrains future drags
accessibility_considerations: Each handle accessibilityLabel='Top-left corner, drag to resize'.
performance_notes: Handles use their own gestures; only x/y/w/h shared values update on drag.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Touch-friendly handles with 32pt hit area. 2) Aspect ratio snapping. 3) Direct manipulation feel.

title: Status bar style management per screen
design_brief: Different screens need different status bar styles: dark text on light background, light text on dark background, transparent for full-bleed hero. Status bar style must update on navigation. Reference: any app with mixed light/dark screens.
mobile_reasoning: expo-status-bar provides StatusBar component; each screen renders its own. Expo Router triggers re-render on focus.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Android needs translucent setup for hero screens.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Style change is instant; OS handles transition.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Each screen sets its own status bar
Hero screens use translucent so content shows through
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: StatusBar is a side-effect component — no render output.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Per-screen status bar matches native screen-by-screen control. 2) Translucent option enables hero designs. 3) System animates the transition.

title: Color contrast modes: outdoor visibility and dark mode
design_brief: App respects system dark mode AND has a separate 'Outdoor' high-contrast mode for use in bright sunlight. Reference: weather apps, fitness apps used outdoors.
mobile_reasoning: useColorScheme for system dark mode + user preference override stored in MMKV. Two color sets per theme: normal and high-contrast. All colors derived from a tokens object that the app reads via context.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: ThemeProvider context. Tokens per theme variant.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Theme switch animates background with timing 200ms.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: User can override mode and contrast
Outdoor mode boosts contrast ratio for sunlight
accessibility_considerations: This task IS the consideration. Outdoor mode hits WCAG AAA contrast.
performance_notes: Theme switching re-renders all themed components — acceptable since rare.
what_makes_this_native: 1) System dark mode integration. 2) Persistent user preference via MMKV. 3) High-contrast variant is real (not just bolder text).

title: FlashList tuning: estimatedItemSize accuracy and getItemType usage
design_brief: Improving a janky list by tuning FlashList configuration: accurate estimatedItemSize, getItemType for mixed cells, drawDistance, and overrideItemLayout. Reference: FlashList docs and real-world tuning sessions.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Mixed cell types without getItemType (recycling bugs)
Tiny drawDistance (visible empty cells during fast scroll)
mobile_reasoning: FlashList's recycling reuses View instances across data. If sizes vary widely without an estimate, layout passes thrash. getItemType ensures different cell shapes (text-only vs media) get separate recycling pools.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Accurate estimate eliminates initial layout jank
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: estimatedItemSize should be the median of actual sizes. getItemType per cell variant. overrideItemLayout gives exact size when known. drawDistance trades memory for scroll smoothness.
what_makes_this_native: 1) FlashList recycling is RN-native pattern. 2) Per-type pools prevent recycling bugs. 3) Drawdistance + accurate estimates = 60fps on Pixel 5.

title: Futurist HUD: monospace, neon cyan, scanlines for data dashboards
design_brief: A crypto / hacking / sci-fi app aesthetic: neon cyan on black, monospace data, animated scan lines and corner brackets. Reference: Blade Runner UI, Severance terminals, Cyberpunk crypto apps.
mobile_reasoning: Single accent (#00FFE0), monospace everywhere, subtle scanlines via Skia, corner bracket frames around data.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Scanlines drift; corner brackets pulse on focus.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Mono at 10pt is borderline — allowFontScaling true.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) textShadow gives glow without extra layers. 2) Custom mono via expo-font. 3) Corner brackets evoke targeting reticles.

title: Segmented control with sliding background indicator
design_brief: An iOS-style segmented control (UISegmentedControl) with a sliding white pill background. Reference: iOS Settings, Apple Music genre tabs.
mobile_reasoning: Background pill is an absolutely-positioned View with shared value driving translateX. Segment widths measured on layout to compute positions.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Slight visual differences but pattern works on both.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Equal-width segments.

animation_strategy: Pill translateX via withSpring.
interaction_details: Pill slides under tapped segment with spring
Text color changes from gray to white
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityRole='tablist'; each Pressable role='tab' state selected.
performance_notes: Single shared value drives pill; no re-render during animation.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Spring-animated indicator. 2) Pixel-identical to iOS UISegmentedControl. 3) Haptic on change.

title: Swipe-down-to-dismiss screen with content fade
design_brief: A photo viewer or detail screen that dismisses on swipe-down with the background fading. Reference: Instagram swipe-down on stories, Twitter image viewer.
mobile_reasoning: Screen content tracks drag with translateY; backdrop opacity interpolated from drag. Past threshold or velocity: call router.back.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: y shared value, backdrop opacity derived.
interaction_details: Drag down: content follows + scales slightly + bg fades
Release past 100px or fast flick: dismisses
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityLabel='Drag down to dismiss' on container.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Drag-to-dismiss is mobile-native (no web equivalent). 2) Scale + fade composition. 3) Velocity-aware dismissal.

title: Bottom sheet with text input that scrolls keyboard out of way
design_brief: A comment composer in a bottom sheet: input at the top of the sheet, when keyboard appears the sheet stays at its snap and the input stays visible (scroll happens within sheet content above input). Reference: Threads reply, Linear comment.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Sheet that shifts when keyboard opens (jumpy)
mobile_reasoning: Use react-native-keyboard-controller to read keyboard height as shared value. Add bottom padding to the scrollable content so it can scroll past the keyboard, but the input bar stays at its fixed position.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: react-native-keyboard-controller unifies behavior.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Keyboard height shared value drives content padding.
interaction_details: Keyboard slides up — composer tracks it
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Hooks are declared once at component top level; mapped marks are plain memoized data, so the Rules of Hooks are preserved.
what_makes_this_native: 1) react-native-keyboard-controller gives UI-thread keyboard height. 2) Composer tracks keyboard frame-for-frame. 3) Scrollable thread accessible while typing.

title: Tab bar that hides on scroll-down, shows on scroll-up
design_brief: A tab bar that translates down off-screen when user scrolls down a list (more reading space), and translates back up when user scrolls up. Reference: Twitter/X, Threads.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hiding tab bar on every scroll event (flickers)
mobile_reasoning: useAnimatedScrollHandler tracks scrollY. Derived value: delta from last scrollY. Past threshold, animate tab bar offscreen. Reset on upward scroll.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: AutoHideTabBar uses scroll handler ref via context.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: tabBarY shared value with withSpring on direction change.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Scroll down past 12px delta + past 80px from top: tab bar slides down
Scroll up: tab bar slides up
accessibility_considerations: VoiceOver users may not scroll fast — keep tab bar accessible via standard rotor.
performance_notes: All scroll logic on UI thread.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Scroll-driven UI on UI thread. 2) Spring physics prevent flicker. 3) Threshold gating avoids twitchy behavior.

title: Number counter animation that ticks up to target value
design_brief: A KPI counter (followers, balance, score) that counts up from 0 to current value over 800ms with deceleration. Reference: Cash App balance, Strava distance counter.
mobile_reasoning: Shared value ticks from 0 to target with deceleration easing. Display text bound via useAnimatedReaction → setState — only fires when display integer changes.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: withTiming easing-out, derived integer.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Counts from previous to new value over 800ms
Deceleration curve — fast start, slow finish
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityLiveRegion='polite' so VoiceOver announces final value (not every tick).
performance_notes: setState only on integer change. Worklet-driven counter.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Worklet counter avoids JS thrash. 2) Tabular nums = no horizontal jitter. 3) Easing curve feels like motion, not animation.

title: Voice memo recorder with waveform during recording
design_brief: A voice memo recorder: tap-and-hold (WhatsApp-style) or tap-to-toggle. Live waveform draws as you record. Reference: WhatsApp voice notes, iOS Voice Memos.
mobile_reasoning: expo-av Recording exposes onRecordingStatusUpdate which gives metering values. Push values to a circular buffer; render as Skia bars.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Microphone permission flow per platform.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Bars push left as new samples come in.
interaction_details: Hold button → medium haptic + recording starts
Max duration enforced (cap in real impl)
accessibility_considerations: Button accessibilityLabel='Hold to record voice memo'. accessibilityHint about release to send.
performance_notes: Metering sampled at 80ms; peaks array capped at 100.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Microphone access via expo-av. 2) Live metering. 3) Hold gesture pattern.

title: Polyline route preview that animates drawing on screen
design_brief: After computing a route, the polyline draws from start to end over 1.2s. Reference: Google Maps directions reveal, Uber pickup route.
mobile_reasoning: Polyline coords array progressively expanded via setState OR map's polyline length animated via Reanimated using a derived coords slice. Map camera animates to route bounds in parallel.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: progress shared value 0→1 with timing.
gesture_strategy: Marker taps move the camera and open a native detail sheet; the same action is available from the result list.
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityLabel on map: 'Route from A to B, X miles'.
performance_notes: Coords slice + setState is the only re-render driver. Polyline native drawing.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Map's fitToCoordinates is native. 2) Polyline is GPU-native. 3) Camera + route animate in parallel.

title: Bar chart with scrub-to-reveal exact values
design_brief: A vertical bar chart (weekly steps, monthly sales). Long-press-drag reveals a vertical crosshair that highlights the bar under finger and shows its value above. Reference: Apple Health steps, Strava weekly.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Permanent value labels above every bar (cluttered)
mobile_reasoning: Skia draws bars; crosshair x is shared value; value text bound via useDerivedValue.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Bar width = (canvasW - totalGap) / count.

animation_strategy: Crosshair opacity via timing.
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityActions for increment/decrement to step through bars.
performance_notes: Hooks are declared once at component top level; mapped marks are plain memoized data, so the Rules of Hooks are preserved.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Tactile scrubbing. 2) GPU drawing. 3) UI-thread interaction.

title: Pull-to-refresh that matches iOS vs Android conventions
design_brief: iOS: pull elastic with circular spinner that grows then spins. Android: pull triggers a Material circular indicator that lifts above content. Use platform-native RefreshControl OR custom that matches conventions.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Threshold that feels wrong on one platform
mobile_reasoning: RefreshControl native component already handles platform conventions correctly. Pass tintColor on iOS, colors on Android.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: This task IS the difference.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Native.
interaction_details: iOS: native elastic pull with growing spinner
Both: refresh callback awaits, sets refreshing state
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native component per platform. 2) Per-platform colors honor system convention. 3) No custom JS for what OS provides.

title: Neumorphism done right: subtle, used sparingly
design_brief: A music player with neumorphic controls — soft inner and outer shadows on a same-color surface to create raised/pressed buttons. Used SPARINGLY on a few key controls, not entire UI. Reference: a handful of well-designed Dribbble pieces (used as inspiration only).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Whole-screen neumorphism (accessibility failure, looks like every Dribbble mock)
mobile_reasoning: Neumorphism requires light and dark shadow simulation. RN doesn't support inner shadows out of the box — use Skia for inner shadow effects on key buttons only. Combine with high-contrast text labels for accessibility.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: NeuButton primitive. Background color chosen carefully.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Buttons large (64-80pt) so the effect is visible.

animation_strategy: Press: switch from raised to pressed shadow state via timing 100ms.
accessibility_considerations: Pair with strong text labels. Avoid using on text inputs.
performance_notes: Shadow only — no Skia needed for the basic version.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Restraint — most neumorphism is overdone. 2) Animated shadow on press. 3) Platform-aware shadow rendering.

title: Toggle switch with on-thumb icon swap
design_brief: A custom switch (not native) where the thumb shows a sun icon when off and a moon when on (light/dark theme toggle). Reference: theme toggles in modern apps.
mobile_reasoning: Track + thumb with thumb's translateX animating. Icons inside thumb crossfade via opacity.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Fixed.

animation_strategy: Thumb translateX, icon opacity.
interaction_details: Track color interpolates from gray to blue
accessibility_considerations: Each input has a label, error hint, visible validation copy, and disabled submit state.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Spring thumb. 2) Color interpolation. 3) Haptic. 4) accessibilityRole='switch' is honored by VoiceOver.

title: Stretchy hero on scroll bounce
design_brief: An iOS-style screen where the hero image stretches as you overscroll up (bounce). Reference: Apple Music album view, Twitter profile.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hero with fixed height regardless of scroll
mobile_reasoning: useAnimatedScrollHandler tracks scrollY. When negative, hero height interpolates from baseline up. Image scale + translateY adjust to keep image filling stretched area.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS only — Android default doesn't bounce.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Scroll-driven shared value.
gesture_strategy: Primary gesture is owned by Gesture Handler on the UI thread and resets with spring physics on release.
interaction_details: Overscroll up: hero stretches and scales
accessibility_considerations: Gesture-only behavior is mirrored by accessibilityActions and a visible label.
performance_notes: All on UI thread.
what_makes_this_native: 1) iOS overscroll bounce is the input. 2) UI-thread interpolation. 3) Apple Music album view exact pattern.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.

title: Avoid bridge thrash via animated props in TextInput
design_brief: A search input that updates a header subtitle live as user types. Naively this requires JS-thread state updates per keystroke. Better: bind text directly to a shared value via Reanimated text props.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Re-rendering the header on every key
mobile_reasoning: useAnimatedProps allows TextInput value bound to shared value via uncontrolled text. For displaying live text elsewhere, use the same shared value via Animated.Text (less common but exists in some workflows).

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: useAnimatedProps for TextInput.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: React state debounced for actual search
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Reanimated handles text without re-rendering on every key.
what_makes_this_native: 1) UI-thread input binding. 2) Debounced React state for expensive ops. 3) Worklet reaction for live UI.

title: Drag-handle bottom sheet with snap-strength visualization
design_brief: As user drags bottom sheet, the drag handle subtly tilts/distorts to communicate gestural commitment direction (toward open or closed snap). Reference: subtle iOS sheet polish.
mobile_reasoning: Handle width and rotation shared values derived from drag velocity sign and magnitude. Reset on snap commit.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: width interpolates from velocity sign; rotation from drag direction.
interaction_details: Fast drag up: handle stretches and tilts up
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Subtle communication of momentum. 2) UI-thread derivation.

title: Photo carousel with native paging and zoom
design_brief: A photo viewer screen showing one photo at a time, swipe horizontally to page through, pinch to zoom (single photo at a time). Reference: Apple Photos, Telegram media viewer.
mobile_reasoning: FlashList horizontal with snapToInterval=SCREEN_WIDTH. Each cell wraps PhotoViewer from rn-025. Pan only enabled when scale > 1.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Width = SCREEN_WIDTH.

animation_strategy: Native paging.
interaction_details: Paging disabled if a photo is zoomed (per inner gesture)
accessibility_considerations: Picker, queue status, and failed upload states are announced with labels and live status text.
performance_notes: FlashList recycles photo viewers. expo-image cache for instant loads.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native paging. 2) Per-photo zoom state. 3) Recycling prevents memory bloat.

title: Date range picker with mini calendar
design_brief: A date range picker (booking apps, analytics filters): mini calendar grid, tap start date, tap end date, range highlighted between. Reference: Airbnb date picker, Google Calendar event range.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Range that snaps weird (e.g., end before start)
mobile_reasoning: Calendar grid as rows of 7 cells. State: startDate, endDate. Cells between are highlighted. Tapping a cell either sets start (if no start) or end (if start set + new is after).

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Cell size scales to fit width / 7.

animation_strategy: Cell selection has subtle scale spring.
interaction_details: Tap once: set start, clear end
Tap again later: set end, range highlighted
accessibility_considerations: Each cell accessibilityRole='button' accessibilityLabel='${date}'. Range edges have state 'selected'.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Direct touch input. 2) Range highlight via background tint. 3) Haptic per selection.

title: Modal stack push with bottom-sheet style transition
design_brief: Pushing a sub-screen from a modal — the new screen slides up from bottom inside the existing modal. Reference: Calendar event detail from create flow.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: New screen pushes via Stack (closes modal first)
Sub-screen as a Modal inside Modal (broken on Android)
mobile_reasoning: Sub-screen managed via in-modal state. Reanimated layout swap with horizontal slide transition.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: translateX between screens.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Nested navigation inside modal context. 2) Maintains modal a11y semantics.

title: FAB that morphs into a full screen
design_brief: A floating action button that on tap morphs into a full-screen detail (its circular bg expands to fill, content fades in). Reference: Material 3 expressive FAB transformations.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Manual border radius animation that pops
mobile_reasoning: FAB position and size animate via shared values. A clone of the FAB is rendered in a portal; while expanding, the original is invisible.

stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: width/height/borderRadius/translateX/Y interpolated.
interaction_details: Tap FAB: expands to full screen with spring
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Spring physics on size + position. 2) Material 3 transformative pattern. 3) Smooth morph without remount.

title: Hermes-specific optimizations and lazy module loading
design_brief: Patterns for reducing app startup time: lazy-load heavy modules, use Hermes' bytecode caching, defer non-critical setup. Reference: Shopify's RN performance blog posts.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Heavy work in App.tsx top level
mobile_reasoning: Hermes pre-compiles JS to bytecode at build time. import() dynamic imports = code-split bundles loaded on demand. Initialization work moved to InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions after first frame.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: First frame paints before heavy work
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hermes bytecode = instant parse. InteractionManager defers past first interaction. Lazy bundles cut initial JS size.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Hermes is RN-specific. 2) InteractionManager is RN-specific. 3) Splash screen control via expo-router.
